summary: Iteration 2 backend testing for Paul Fitness Gym manager CRUD endpoints (walk-in create, member update, member delete with cascade). Created /app/backend/tests/test_manager_member_crud.py with 18 new pytest cases. All 18/18 PASSED. Full suite (iteration-1 + iteration-2) = 35/35 PASSED. Verified: walk-in create with auto-generated email when not provided (walkin_<hex>@paulfitness.local, role=user, walk_in=true), explicit email respected (lowercased), duplicate email returns 409, missing name OR phone returns 400/422 (Pydantic validator), no token returns 401. PATCH updates name+phone and returns full user; empty body -> 400; invalid id -> 404; no token -> 401. DELETE cascades: pre-seeded a user_session and push_token plus an active membership (via record-cash) for a walk-in, then DELETE returned 200 and verified all four collections (users, memberships, user_sessions, push_tokens) had count=0 for that user_id via direct Mongo. Non-existent delete -> 404, no token -> 401. Full e2e flow passed: walk-in -> cash (plan_3m Quarterly) -> detail (current=active, no _id leak) -> patch name -> delete -> GET 404. No _id leakage in any of the new endpoints' responses (user, membership, history). Regression smoke: /api/plans still 4, /api/manager/stats keys intact, /api/manager/members list no _id leak.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py /api/manager/members (POST walk-in): missing-name/missing-phone returns 422 (Pydantic) when fields absent and 400 only when they're present-but-blank. Acceptable, but consider Field(min_length=1) + a single 422 path for consistency.

server.py /api/manager/members (POST): synthesized walk-in email uses `@paulfitness.local`; this is unique enough but if a real walk-in later signs in with Google, the placeholder won't auto-link. Consider a 'link walk-in' flow later.

server.py PATCH /api/manager/members/{user_id}: `payload.name is not None and payload.name.strip()` silently drops blank-name updates without 400. Currently masked because empty body already returns 400, but sending {"name": "   "} returns 400 too only because nothing else updates. Minor.
server.py DELETE /api/manager/members/{user_id}: cascade is well-implemented (users, memberships, user_sessions, push_tokens). Consider also deleting payment_orders for completeness.
Pre-existing: @app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown') is deprecated; migrate to lifespan handler.
Pre-existing: /api/payments/order only requires get_user_by_token (any role); add role=='user' guard before enabling Razorpay.
seed_data_creation: Per-test walk-in members created via POST /api/manager/members (tracked in module-level list and torn down after the module). For cascade test, manually seeded one user_sessions row and one push_tokens row directly in Mongo, then asserted they were removed after DELETE.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Two pytest files now: test_backend_api.py (iteration 1, 17 tests) and test_manager_member_crud.py (iteration 2, 18 tests). Run `pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v` for full regression. Uses EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env and MONGO_URL/DB_NAME from /app/backend/.env. Manager creds in /app/memory/test_credentials.md. Walk-in members are POST /api/manager/members and get walk_in=true, role=user; cascade DELETE removes users+memberships+user_sessions+push_tokens (NOT payment_orders).
